key_findings: The codebase has 8 agents, 35 skills, and 3 commands per manifest.json, but every documentation file still cites v1.0.0 figures of 5-6 agents, 32-33 skills, and 1 command.

The autonomous debugging subsystem (debugger agent, auto-debug command, debug-diagnostics skill, .debug/ session directory) and autonomous audit subsystem (auditor agent, auto-audit command, spec-compliance skill) have zero documentation coverage across all user-facing files.
SECURITY.md Best Practices and Audit Trail sections still reference ~/.claude/sessions/ as the checkpoint location, which migrated to project-local .orchestrate/<session-id>/ post-v1.0.0.
CONTRIBUTING.md contains a broken link to claude-code/COOKBOOK.md which does not exist in the repository.
PERMISSION-MODES.md agent compatibility matrix is missing rows for researcher, debugger, and auditor agents.
CHANGELOG.md and RELEASE-NOTES.md Unreleased sections are missing changelog entries for all post-v1.0.0 additions (debugger, auditor, auto-debug, auto-audit, debug-diagnostics, spec-compliance).
Industry best practice for multi-agent systems confirms per-agent compatibility matrices and decision flow diagrams as correct approach; the documentation gaps are content gaps, not format problems.
